title: Speedboat Hire in Estepona Port: Costs, Licences and Tips for 2026
html:
Speedboat hire in Estepona Port offers an exhilarating way to explore the Costa del Sol, with rentals starting around €250 for a half-day on a 6-metre vessel. Departing from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, you can reach Gibraltar in under 30 minutes. BoatHire24 connects you with local owners for verified boat rental Estepona Port options.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in Estepona Port?

Half-day rentals typically range from €200 to €600 depending on boat size and season. A 4.9-metre Olympic 490 with a 90 HP engine costs about €250 for four hours in low season, while a larger 6-metre Quicksilver Activ 605 with 150 HP might run €450. Full-day rates (8 hours) usually double the half-day price. For the best deals, compare verified listings on boat rental Estepona Port.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Estepona Port?

Spain requires a recreational boating licence for most powerboats of 15 HP or more. Non-EU visitors can use an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a valid national licence. The minimum age is 16 for boats under 40 HP, and 18 for more powerful craft. Always confirm specific requirements with your operator before booking.

Where to set off in Estepona Port

Puerto Deportivo de Estepona is the main departure marina, offering sheltered berths and fuel dock. Nearby, Puerto Banús is a glamorous alternative 15 minutes east. From Estepona, head to the protected waters of the Bay of Algeciras, or cruise west to the Sotogrande marina. For a longer trip, the Gibraltar Strait is a highlight, where you can see the Rock of Gibraltar. Further afield, Marbella’s coastline is a popular 20-minute run.

Which boat to rent

  1. Olympic 490, Best for couples exploring local coves; 4 guests max; from €250 half-day. See the Olympic 490 listing for full specs.
  2. Quicksilver Activ 605 Open, Ideal for families or groups of 6; 150 HP engine; from €450 half-day.
  3. Axopar 28, A premium crossover perfect for day trips to Gibraltar; 8 guests; from €600 half-day.
  4. Jeanneau Merry Fisher 795, Comfortable for up to 7, with a cabin; from €550 half-day.

Cost breakdown for speedboat hire

Prices in euros are typical for 2026 and vary by season and boat size.

  • Shared tour (4 hours, max 10 people): €80 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, 6 guests): €250, €400.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, 12 guests): €1,200, €2,000.
  • Crewed yacht (8 hours, 8 guests): €1,500, €2,500.

Licence and season tips

High season runs from June to September, when the Levante wind can pick up in the afternoons. Booking early morning ensures calmer seas. The best months for stable weather are May and October. Always carry a valid licence, as local Guardia Civil patrols check paperwork regularly.

If you are renting without a licence, some skippers offer a ‘bareboat with skipper’ option: you pay extra for a local captain who handles navigation while you relax.

At a glance

  • Price band: €250, €2,500 per trip
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: 4 or 8 hours
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Estepona Port

Can I rent a speedboat for a day trip to Gibraltar?

Yes, many operators allow you to cruise the 20 nautical miles from Estepona Port to Gibraltar, but you must clear customs and port authorities before entering British waters.

What is the cheapest speedboat hire option?

Shared tours from Estepona Port start at €80 per person for a four-hour trip, offering an affordable way to experience the coast.

Is fuel included in the rental price?

Fuel is usually not included; expect to pay around €50, €100 for a half-day’s cruising, depending on engine size and distance.

Do I need a VHF radio licence?

A VHF licence is not mandatory for private rentals in Spain, but your operator may provide one for safety communications.
